step_by_step_thinking: Selinexor is explicitly listed among treatment options for relapsed and refractory multiple myeloma, specifically in cases of triple-class refractory disease and when other agents have failed. Its inclusion in specific regimens—such as SVd (selinexor, bortezomib, and dexamethasone)—in studies like the BOSTON Trial confirms its efficacy in clinical practice. Additionally, it is referenced alongside other targeted agents and as part of viable alternatives in advanced-stage disease. Therefore, selinexor is recognized as an active and clinically valid therapeutic agent for multiple myeloma.
raw_response:
Selinexor is explicitly listed among treatment options for relapsed and refractory multiple myeloma, specifically in cases of triple-class refractory disease and when other agents have failed. Its inclusion in specific regimens—such as SVd (selinexor, bortezomib, and dexamethasone)—in studies like the BOSTON Trial confirms its efficacy in clinical practice. Additionally, it is referenced alongside other targeted agents and as part of viable alternatives in advanced-stage disease. Therefore, selinexor is recognized as an active and clinically valid therapeutic agent for multiple myeloma.
\boxed{A}
